message:
Implement SQLite storage layer with schema v1 and migrations

Add complete rusqlite-based persistence implementation providing schema
initialization, performance tuning, migration system, and CRUD operations
for normalized conversation data.

SqliteStorage struct and lifecycle:
Public API entry point is SqliteStorage::open(path) which:
1. Creates parent directories if missing (fs::create_dir_all) to handle
   XDG data directory initialization (e.g., ~/.local/share/coding-agent-
   search/ on first run)
2. Opens SQLite connection via rusqlite::Connection::open (bundled
   feature ensures FTS5 availability per Cargo.toml)
3. Applies performance pragmas (apply_pragmas): WAL mode for concurrent
   readers during indexing, synchronous=NORMAL for 2-3x faster writes
   vs FULL durability, temp_store=MEMORY for sort/index operations,
   cache_size=-65536 (64MB page cache), mmap_size=256MB for memory-
   mapped I/O, foreign_keys=ON for referential integrity
4. Initializes meta table and schema_version tracking (init_meta)
5. Runs migrations if needed (migrate): executes MIGRATION_V1 SQL on
   first run or schema version mismatch

Schema design (MIGRATION_V1 DDL):
Seven-table normalized model per PLAN § 4.2:
- meta: key-value store for schema_version and future app metadata
- agents: tool registry (slug, name, version, kind) with UNIQUE(slug)
  for upsert operations, created_at/updated_at timestamps
- workspaces: project root paths with optional display_name, UNIQUE(path)
- conversations: core table linking agent_id and optional workspace_id,
  external_id for source tool's session identifier, title for UI display,
  source_path to original log file, started_at/ended_at timestamps,
  approx_tokens for context estimation, metadata_json TEXT for tool-
  specific fields, UNIQUE(agent_id, external_id) constraint enabling
  idempotent rescans (per PLAN § 7.1 connector deduplication)
- messages: individual turns within conversation, idx for ordering,
  role/author/created_at/content/extra_json fields, ON DELETE CASCADE
  foreign key to conversations, UNIQUE(conversation_id, idx) preventing
  duplicate messages
- snippets: code fragment references attached to messages, optional
  file_path/start_line/end_line/language/snippet_text, ON DELETE CASCADE
  to messages
- tags: classification labels with UNIQUE(name)
- conversation_tags: many-to-many join table with composite primary key

Index strategy for query performance:
Three composite/covering indexes per PLAN § 4.3:
- idx_conversations_agent_started: (agent_id, started_at DESC) for
  efficient "list recent conversations per agent" queries in UI results
  pane, DESC sort order matches display order (newest first)
- idx_messages_conv_idx: (conversation_id, idx) for ordered message
  fetch when loading conversation detail pane, leverages UNIQUE
  constraint as covering index
- idx_messages_created: (created_at) for time-range filtering across
  all conversations ("show messages from last 7 days")

Performance pragmas explained:
- journal_mode=WAL: Write-Ahead Logging allows readers to proceed while
  writer commits, critical for UI responsiveness during background
  indexing. Writer appends to -wal file, readers see snapshot from
  last checkpoint. Checkpoint occurs automatically at 1000 page
  threshold or can be triggered manually via PRAGMA wal_checkpoint
- synchronous=NORMAL: fsync() only at critical checkpoints (not every
  transaction), trading durability for 2-3x write throughput. Acceptable
  for derived data (source logs are canonical, SQLite can be rebuilt
  from connectors if corrupted)
- temp_store=MEMORY: Use heap for temporary tables and indexes during
  sort/group operations instead of writing to disk, faster for systems
  with adequate RAM (assumes 8GB+ per PLAN § 5.4 hardware targets)
- cache_size=-65536: Negative value interpreted as KB (64MB), stores
  hot pages in memory to reduce disk I/O during sequential scans of
  messages table
- mmap_size=256MB: Memory-map database file up to 256MB, enabling
  direct memory access without system calls for read-heavy queries
  (most TUI interactions are reads, writes only during indexing)
- foreign_keys=ON: Enforce ON DELETE CASCADE constraints so deleting
  conversation automatically removes child messages/snippets, prevents
  orphaned rows

Migration system (SCHEMA_VERSION constant):
Current version is 1 (MIGRATION_V1). Future schema changes will:
1. Increment SCHEMA_VERSION to 2
2. Add MIGRATION_V2 constant with ALTER TABLE statements or data
   transformations (e.g., "ALTER TABLE messages ADD COLUMN embedding BLOB"
   for future semantic search)
3. Update migrate() function to handle current == 1 case: execute
   MIGRATION_V2, update meta.schema_version to 2
4. If schema incompatible with Tantivy index (field renamed/removed),
   indexer orchestration layer will detect version bump and trigger
   full Tantivy rebuild from SQLite (per PLAN § 8.3 migration
   coordination)

CRUD operations implemented:
- ensure_agent(Agent) -> Result<i64>: Upsert agent row via INSERT...ON
  CONFLICT DO UPDATE, returns agent.id for foreign key. Updates name,
  version, kind, updated_at if slug already exists (handles version
  bumps across application runs), inserts new row if first time seeing
  agent. Uses now_millis() helper for timestamp consistency.

- ensure_workspace(path, display_name) -> Result<i64>: Upsert workspace
  via INSERT...ON CONFLICT with COALESCE to preserve existing display_name
  if not provided (avoids overwriting user-customized names). Returns
  workspace.id for conversations foreign key.

- insert_conversation_tree(agent_id, workspace_id, Conversation) ->
  Result<i64>: Transaction-wrapped batch insert for full conversation
  including all messages and snippets. Checks for existing conversation
  via (agent_id, external_id) query first—if found, returns existing ID
  without re-inserting (idempotent scans per PLAN § 7.1). Otherwise:
  1. INSERT conversation row (insert_conversation helper)
  2. For each message: INSERT message row, capture message.id
     (insert_message helper)
  3. For each snippet in message: INSERT snippet row referencing message.id
     (insert_snippets helper)
  4. COMMIT transaction (all-or-nothing: conversation with all children
     or rollback on any error)

Helper functions for field serialization:
- path_to_string(PathBuf) -> String: Convert filesystem paths to UTF-8
  lossy strings for TEXT column storage, handles non-UTF8 paths on Unix
  by replacing invalid sequences with � (acceptable since paths are
  primarily for display/logging, not reverse reconstruction)
- role_str(MessageRole) -> String: Map enum to canonical lowercase
  strings ("user", "agent", "tool", "system", or passthrough for Other
  variant). Inverse mapping (string -> MessageRole) happens in query
  functions (not implemented yet, future commit).
- agent_kind_str(AgentKind) -> String: Map Cli/VsCode/Hybrid to lowercase
  strings for storage
- now_millis() -> i64: Unix epoch milliseconds via SystemTime, consistent
  timestamp format across all tables

Error handling with anyhow context:
All database operations wrapped in anyhow::Result, use .context() or
.with_context() to add human-readable error messages preserving
underlying rusqlite error. Examples:
- "opening sqlite db at /path/to/db.sqlite" on Connection::open failure
- "creating db directory /parent/path" on fs::create_dir_all failure
- "fetching agent id for codex" on query_row failure after insert
- "unsupported schema version 99" on migration version mismatch
These context strings appear in TUI error screens and logs, helping
users diagnose issues (e.g., permission denied on db directory, disk
full during write).

Transaction usage pattern:
insert_conversation_tree uses explicit transaction (conn.transaction()?)
rather than auto-commit mode to ensure atomicity: either all rows
inserted successfully or none (prevents partial conversation with
messages but no snippets if snippet insert fails midway). Transactions
also batch writes for performance: single fsync at commit instead of
per-statement fsync in default mode.

Deferred: FTS5 virtual table, query functions, conversation/message fetch:
This commit implements schema + write path only. Future commits will add:
- FTS5 virtual table creation and sync (per PLAN § 4.4) for fallback
  text search when Tantivy disabled
- Query APIs: fetch_conversation_by_id, list_recent_conversations,
  search_fts5 (per PLAN § 3.1 storage responsibilities)
- Conversation reconstruction from database rows (JOIN messages, snippets,
  map role strings back to MessageRole enum)

Testing strategy (future commit):
Unit tests will use rusqlite::Connection::open_in_memory() to create
ephemeral database, run migrations, insert test data, assert schema
correctness and UNIQUE constraint enforcement. Integration tests will
use tempfile::tempdir() for filesystem-backed database to verify
directory creation and WAL file handling.

Related: PLAN_TO_MAKE_CODING_AGENT_SESSION_SEARCH.md § 4.2 (schema),
§ 4.3 (pragmas), § 8.3 (migrations), model/types.rs (Conversation/Message/
Snippet types), connectors/mod.rs (NormalizedConversation input format),
Cargo.toml (rusqlite bundled+modern_sqlite features).
